Where there is no direct access to the rear of the lot or the Director Engineering is satisfied that access to the rear is not feasible because of an extreme grade, parking may be provided elsewhere on the lot.

condition: All dwelling units
subject: direct pedestrian access
original_excerpt: (a) provides direct pedestrian access from the dwelling unit entrance to a lot line abutting a street, or abutting a constructed lane where direct access to a street is not feasible; and

condition: All dwelling units; must be clear of any projections or obstructions
subject: clear height
original_excerpt: (b) is clear of any projections or obstructions to a minimum of 2.5 m in height.

applies_to: panhandle lots and other irregularly shaped lots
condition: Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: panhandle lots and other irregularly shaped lots
original_excerpt: (a) panhandle lots and other irregularly shaped lots may be permitted subject to the following:

condition: Lots with lane access; Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: panhandle width
original_excerpt: (i) lots with lane access shall have a minimum panhandle width of 1 m that is clear to a height of 2.5 m; and

condition: Lots with lane access; Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: clear height
original_excerpt: (i) lots with lane access shall have a minimum panhandle width of 1 m that is clear to a height of 2.5 m; and

condition: Lots without lane access; Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: panhandle width
original_excerpt: (ii) lots without lane access shall have a minimum panhandle width of 4.5 m that is clear to a height of 2.5 m;

condition: Lots without lane access; Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: clear height
original_excerpt: (ii) lots without lane access shall have a minimum panhandle width of 4.5 m that is clear to a height of 2.5 m;

condition: Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: lot coverage
original_excerpt: (b) maximum lot coverage as set out in Section 101.4 may be increased up to 60% and impervious surface area up to 70%;

condition: Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: impervious surface area
original_excerpt: (b) maximum lot coverage as set out in Section 101.4 may be increased up to 60% and impervious surface area up to 70%;

condition: Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: original architectural appurtenances
original_excerpt: (c) all original architectural appurtenances, such as chimneys, railings, vents, decorative features, or similar, may be excluded from the maximum permitted height of a principal building;

condition: Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: lot line setbacks
original_excerpt: (d) lot line setbacks for street yards may meet a minimum of 2.0 m;

condition: Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: minimum separation between buildings
original_excerpt: (e) the minimum separation between buildings on the same lot as required under Section 101.4 may be reduced;

condition: Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: Section 101.5.1
original_excerpt: (f) Section 101.5.1 does not apply; and

condition: Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: off-street vehicular parking spaces
original_excerpt: (i) off-street vehicular parking spaces are not required;

condition: Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: retention and continued use of non-conforming driveways
original_excerpt: (ii) retention and continued use of driveways that do not otherwise conform with the requirements of this Bylaw may be permitted; and

condition: Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: parking within required yards
original_excerpt: (iii) parking may be provided within the required yards, and any existing parking areas on the lot that do not conform with the requirements of this Bylaw may continue to be used.

condition: Lots in the R1 District on the Community Heritage Register, subject to approval of the Director of Planning and Development and registration of a Section 219 Covenant protecting the heritage resource
subject: continued use of non-conforming parking areas
original_excerpt: (iii) parking may be provided within the required yards, and any existing parking areas on the lot that do not conform with the requirements of this Bylaw may continue to be used.

value: lower of the average front or average rear elevation, as defined by the lower of the average natural grade or finished grade along the exterior of the building facing the respective front or rear lot line
subject: height
original_excerpt: The height of a principal or accessory building shall be measured from the lower of the average front or average rear elevation, as defined by the lower of the average natural grade or finished grade along the exterior of the building facing the respective front or rear lot line

value: midpoint of a sloped roof or the highest point of a flat the roof
subject: height
original_excerpt: to the midpoint of a sloped roof or the highest point of a flat the roof.

value: one half of the vertical distance of a sloped roof structure measured from the top plate of the uppermost storey to the highest point of the roof
subject: midpoint
original_excerpt: The midpoint of a sloped roof is considered to be one half of the vertical distance of a sloped roof structure measured from the top plate of the uppermost storey to the highest point of the roof.

value: finished grade at the point used for vehicular access
subject: height
original_excerpt: The height of a detached garage or carport shall be measured from the finished grade at the point used for vehicular access.

value: nearest point of each building face
subject: separation distances
original_excerpt: Required separation distances between buildings are measured from the nearest point of each building face.

subject: Supplementary Regulations
original_excerpt: (a) Section 6: Supplementary Regulations

subject: Off-Street Parking
original_excerpt: (b) Schedule 8: Off-Street Parking
